name: Database Session Manager
description: Provides async SQLAlchemy database session management using a connection pool. Exposes a generator function to yield database sessions with automatic lifecycle management (commit/rollback/close). Used as a dependency injection target in FastAPI endpoints.

types.name: AsyncSession
types.description: SQLAlchemy async database session type from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio

types.name: AsyncGenerator[AsyncSession, None]
types.description: Async generator type that yields AsyncSession instances

functions.name: get_db
functions.description: Async generator that yields a SQLAlchemy AsyncSession with automatic lifecycle management. Sessions expire on close but not on commit (expire_on_commit=False). Used as a dependency injection target in FastAPI endpoints.

error_cases.name: DatabaseConnectionError
error_cases.condition: Database URL is invalid or database is unreachable

error_cases.name: DatabaseConfigurationError
error_cases.condition: settings.database_url is not set or malformed

preconditions: settings.database_url must be configured and accessible
Database engine must be initialized successfully
postconditions: Session is automatically closed after context exit
Session objects do not expire on commit
Uncommitted transactions are rolled back on context exit
side_effects: Establishes database connection from connection pool

structured_side_effects.description: Establishes database connection from connection pool

invariants: engine is initialized once at module load with echo=False
Each invocation of get_db() yields a new session instance from the factory

data_access.rationale: Provides database session management with read/write access through SQLAlchemy AsyncSession. Manages transaction lifecycle including commits and rollbacks.

side_effects.rationale: Establishes and manages database connections from a connection pool
Automatically manages transaction lifecycle based on context exit conditions
